verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is canonically a secreted serine protease that lacks a transmembrane domain, and its dominant biology involves secretion into prostatic fluid and peripheral circulation. However, multiple recent studies (Shen et al., Clin Cancer Res 2025; Phase 0 radioligand trial) have confirmed documented cell surface expression in AR+ prostate cancer cells and patient-derived mCRPC tumor tissue by FACS and immunofluorescence. The surface pool is tissue- and cancer-state-restricted (AR-driven, prostate-specific), and KLK2 is being actively targeted by bispecific T-cell engagers (pasritamig), CAR-T (JNJ-75229414), radioligand therapy (225Ac-DOTA-h11B6), and ADC modalities against the cell-surface form. The mechanism of surface retention is not fully characterized, but expression at the outer face in prostate cancer cells is experimentally confirmed and therapeutically validated across multiple modalities. This qualifies as contextual (cell-state/tissue-restricted surface expression in prostate cancer cells) rather than constitutive baseline surface localization.
